arts.ttl: Monza’s Transfer Market Heats Up: Negotiations with AC Milan for Ballo-Touré and Origi
arts.sum: Monza's transfer market activities are intensifying as the club seeks to strengthen its squad for the second half of the season. The team has initiated contact with AC Milan regarding potential moves for Fodé Ballo-Touré and Divock Origi, signaling their intent to bolster both defensive and attacking options. arts.ttl: Parma Step Up Pursuit of Martin Erlic from Bologna
arts.sum: Parma are ramping up their efforts to secure the services of Martin Erlic, the Croatian defender currently with Bologna. The newly promoted side are keen to bolster their defensive lineup and see Erlic as a valuable addition. Gianluca Di Marzio reports that negotiations between Parma and Bologna are reportedly progressing well, with the transfer package for Erlic estimated to be around €7 million, including potential bonuses. As discussions continue, both clubs are working to finalize the details of the deal. The situation is expected to evolve quickly, especially with Bologna’s upcoming match on January 15.
